response:
To determine the general formula for the number of pages read on the nth day we must analyze the given quadratic sequence. The problem states that the sequence is quadratic meaning the second differences between consecutive terms are constant. The first three terms are five pages on day one twelve pages on day two and twenty one pages on day three.

First we calculate the first differences between consecutive terms. The difference between day two and day one is twelve minus five which equals seven. The difference between day three and day two is twenty one minus twelve which equals nine. These first differences are seven and nine. Next we calculate the second difference by subtracting the first difference from the second difference. Nine minus seven equals two. Since the second difference is constant at two the sequence is indeed quadratic.

A general quadratic sequence can be expressed in the form P(n) equals an squared plus bn plus c. The coefficient a is determined by half of the second difference. Since the second difference is two the value of a is one. We now have the partial formula P(n) equals n squared plus bn plus c. To find b and c we substitute the known values for n equals one and n equals two.

For n equals one the total pages read is five. Substituting into the equation we get one squared plus b times one plus c equals five. This simplifies to one plus b plus c equals five. Subtracting one from both sides gives b plus c equals four. For n equals two the total pages read is twelve. Substituting into the equation we get two squared plus b times two plus c equals twelve. This simplifies to four plus two b plus c equals twelve. Subtracting four from both sides gives two b plus c equals eight.

We now have a system of two linear equations. The first is b plus c equals four. The second is two b plus c equals eight. Subtracting the first equation from the second eliminates c. Two b minus b equals b. Eight minus four equals four. Therefore b equals four. Substituting b equals four back into the first equation gives four plus c equals four. Subtracting four from both sides reveals that c equals zero.

The general formula for the number of pages read on the nth day is P(n) equals n squared plus four n. We can verify this formula with the third term. For n equals three the calculation is three squared plus four times three. This equals nine plus twelve which is twenty one. This matches the given data confirming the formula is correct.
